input: I have been closely monitoring your account over the past eight months and I can see that we have executed 47 transactions in your equity portfolio, including 22 round-trip trades where we bought and sold the same security within a short period. This level of activity has generated substantial brokerage commissions for our firm. The portfolio performance net of these costs has lagged the Hang Seng Index by 12% over the same period. I want to assure you that each trade was individually justified based on market conditions at the time.

Before the company's earnings announcement next Tuesday, I placed buy orders for 50,000 shares at the open today. I had an indication from a contact close to management that the numbers were going to beat consensus estimates. Once the announcement came out and the stock moved up 8%, I sold the position at the intraday peak for a profit of approximately HK$320,000. The position was closed before market close on announcement day.

Our trading desk executed a series of coordinated buy and sell orders in Greenfield Resources shares over a 10-day period to maintain an artificially elevated price ahead of a planned secondary placement. The orders were designed to create a false appearance of active trading interest in the stock, supporting the placement price. The strategy involved cross-desk coordination to prevent any net position from accumulating while maintaining visible turnover.

We are launching a new scheme called the HK Property Income Trust, which will pool investor capital to acquire residential properties in Hong Kong and distribute rental income quarterly. Shares in the scheme can be purchased starting from HK$10,000 and will be available to the general public through our online platform. The scheme is not currently registered with or authorised by the Securities and Futures Commission, but we believe our structure falls outside the regulatory definition of a collective investment scheme.

As your portfolio manager, I identified an urgent opportunity last Thursday that required immediate action before markets closed. Rather than waiting for your confirmation, I executed a purchase of HK$2.8 million in a high-yield bond on your behalf under what I determined was my discretionary authority. However, I should clarify that your account mandate is advisory only, and I have not received written authorisation from you to exercise discretion over your account. I am disclosing this to you now.

I have transferred HK$1.5 million from your segregated client account to our firm's operating account to cover a short-term cash flow requirement. This was a temporary measure and the funds will be returned by end of month. Your investment positions remain unchanged and you will not see any impact on your portfolio valuation. We have made a note in our internal records. This kind of short-term internal borrowing is a standard practice in the industry.

We offer convenient business loans starting from HK$50,000 with flexible repayment terms up to 36 months. Our effective interest rate is 18% per annum on a flat basis. We do not hold a money lender's licence from the Licensing Court, but our structure is designed as a service fee arrangement rather than a loan agreement, which we believe means the Money Lenders Ordinance does not apply. Apply today with just a business registration certificate and your last three months of bank statements.

We have completed onboarding for a new corporate client that lists its ultimate beneficial owner as a resident of a FATF-blacklisted jurisdiction. Our CDD file contains the customer's certificate of incorporation and a signed self-declaration from the company secretary confirming ownership structure. No PEP screening was conducted because our compliance team assessed this client as lower risk based on the modest initial transaction volume. The account has been approved by a relationship manager without compliance sign-off.

Our flagship Dragon Alpha Fund has delivered a compound annual return of 34% since inception. These returns have been independently verified and reflect the performance of actual client accounts. What we do not mention in this marketing material is that our performance figures reflect only the top-quartile accounts from our client base; accounts that were closed due to losses are excluded from the calculation. We believe this represents our strategy's best potential outcomes for prospective clients.

Join our 12-month wealth growth programme. Participants contribute HK$50,000 monthly and receive guaranteed distributions of 3% per month — that is 36% per year — funded by returns generated from a proprietary global macro trading strategy. Early participants have already been receiving their monthly distributions as promised. The more participants that join, the stronger our combined capital pool and the better the returns we can deliver for everyone in the programme.
